C3-3-TiMe2.BHT_effect: BHT can reduce MAO demand and increase molecular weight in screening experiments.
notes: Low pressure around 0.7 MPa retains activity and favors ENB incorporation.
Increasing ENB feed lowers activity moderately while product ENB increases.
Raising pressure toward 2 MPa reduces product ENB content.

D2-2-TiCl2.BHT_effect: Use BHT as an optional molecular-weight and MAO-demand modifier; calibration data still limited.
notes: High-throughput screening showed good activity at 0.7 MPa and high molecular weight.
Product ENB rises approximately linearly with ENB feed.
High ENB products can still maintain useful ENB conversion.

CGC-like.BHT_effect: BHT helps reduce MAO ratio and can increase Mw; validate by dedicated BHT/AlTi DOE.
notes: MAO reduction from Al/Ti 500 to 200 can cause clear activity loss.
At 2 MPa, ENB conversion is typically lower than at 0.7 MPa.
Observed monomer conversion ranking is generally ethylene > ENB > propylene.

mono-metallocene-like.BHT_effect: BHT effect should be calibrated; use as a screened modifier rather than a fixed design rule.
notes: Low pressure around 0.7 MPa is preferred for ENB incorporation.
High pressure around 2 MPa reduces ENB content and conversion.
Use for high-ENB and fast-cure EPDM screening windows.

PDF_rules.BHT_effect: BHT can reduce MAO requirement and increase Mw; hydrotuning still needs dedicated experiments.
notes: Low pressure 0.7 MPa is beneficial for ENB incorporation; high pressure 2 MPa lowers ENB content.
MAO Al/Ti ratio reduction from 500 to 200 causes obvious activity decrease.
Increasing ENB feed raises product ENB approximately linearly but ENB conversion decreases.
Overall conversion ranking in kettle data: ethylene > ENB > propylene.
E/P ratio changes product composition and discharge/fouling state.
2 L and 5 L kettle runs have similar activity and gel state; 5 L tends toward higher Mw, broader PDI and lower Mooney.
